extension.valueMarkdown:
## Character
51-year-old Hispanic male, high school shop teacher and baseball coach. Lives in San Antonio, TX with his wife Rosa and youngest daughter (age 16). Born in San Antonio, lifelong Texan. Insurance: employer plan through the school district (Aetna). Active lifestyle compromised by progressive joint disease.

## Clinical Scenario
Carlos was diagnosed with seropositive rheumatoid arthritis in 2021 after 6 months of progressive symmetric polyarthritis (hands, wrists, feet). His PCP noted elevated inflammatory markers and referred him to a rheumatologist. Initial workup showed positive RF (78 IU/mL), positive anti-CCP (>250 U/mL), elevated ESR (42) and CRP (2.8), and erosive changes on hand X-rays.

He was started on methotrexate 15mg weekly with folic acid. After 4 months of adequate dosing, his DAS28-CRP remained elevated at 4.2 (moderate disease activity). Methotrexate was increased to 20mg weekly. At 6 months on 20mg, he developed elevated liver enzymes (ALT 98, AST 82) — methotrexate was discontinued for hepatotoxicity.

He was switched to leflunomide 20mg daily. After 6 months on leflunomide, his DAS28-CRP was 4.8 (moderate-high), with worsening hand grip strength and new wrist erosions on follow-up imaging. Leflunomide was deemed an inadequate response.

His rheumatologist recommends initiating adalimumab (Humira biosimilar — Hadlima) as step-up biologic therapy. The prior authorization requires documentation of: RA diagnosis, failed conventional DMARDs (methotrexate — hepatotoxicity; leflunomide — inadequate response), disease activity scores, functional status, and imaging showing progression.

Aetna uses a Permission Ticket to access Carlos's rheumatology and PCP records to adjudicate the prior authorization request.

## Sites
- **Alamo Family Practice** — PCP in San Antonio, TX (2015–present). General primary care, initial joint complaint workup, referral to rheumatology, routine management.
- **South Texas Rheumatology** — Rheumatologist in San Antonio, TX (2021–present). RA diagnosis, DMARD management, disease activity monitoring, biologic prior auth documentation.

Two sites, one state (TX). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on **scopes** (payer needs Condition, Observation [disease activity scores, labs], DiagnosticReport [imaging], MedicationRequest [DMARD history], Procedure — focused on RA treatment) and **periods** (payer needs the full DMARD treatment history from 2021–present).

resource.conclusion: Clear lungs. No infiltrates or nodules. Ordered as pre-treatment screening prior to methotrexate initiation.
Periarticular osteopenia at MTPs. No erosions identified. Mild soft tissue swelling. No acute osseous abnormality.

Periarticular osteopenia at MCPs bilaterally. Early marginal erosions at 2nd and 3rd MCP joints of the right hand. No joint space narrowing. Soft tissue swelling at bilateral MCPs and wrists. Findings consistent with early erosive rheumatoid arthritis.

code.text: Bilateral hand radiographs, PA and oblique views

resource.conclusion: 1. Interval progression of erosive rheumatoid arthritis with new marginal erosions bilaterally (right 4th MCP, left 2nd MCP) and worsening of previously documented erosions at the right 2nd and 3rd MCP joints, compared to March 15, 2021 baseline study. 2. New joint space narrowing at the right 2nd MCP joint and bilateral radiocarpal joints, indicating cartilage loss. 3. Persistent periarticular osteopenia and soft tissue swelling bilaterally. 4. Findings indicate inadequate disease control with structural progression despite current DMARD therapy.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 mg by mouth once daily

statusReason.text: Hepatotoxicity — ALT 98 U/L and AST 82 U/L, more than twice upper limit of normal

dosageInstruction.text: Take 20 mg by mouth once weekly

priorPrescription.display: methotrexate 15 mg oral tablet, once weekly (initiated 2021-03-15)

note.text: Dose increased from 15 mg to 20 mg weekly due to persistent moderate disease activity (DAS28-CRP 4.2, not at target of <3.2). Good tolerability, no GI symptoms, normal LFTs.
DISCONTINUED due to methotrexate hepatotoxicity. ALT 98 U/L and AST 82 U/L (>2x ULN), progressive increase from September 2021 values. Patient symptomatic with nausea and fatigue.
Bridge NSAID therapy for symptomatic relief while awaiting DMARD effect

dosageInstruction.text: Take 500 mg by mouth twice daily

note.text: Planned to initiate at next visit (January 2022), contingent on confirmation of full LFT normalization. Alternative DMARD after methotrexate failure due to hepatotoxicity.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily.

note.text: Second conventional DMARD trial. Loading dose intentionally omitted (no 100 mg x 3 day protocol) due to recent methotrexate-induced hepatotoxicity and to reduce GI side effect risk. If leflunomide proves inadequate, next step is biologic therapy requiring prior authorization.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily. No loading dose.
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily.

coding.display: omeprazole 20 MG Delayed Release Oral Capsule

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 capsule (20 mg) by mouth once daily, 30 minutes before breakfast
dosageInstruction.patientInstruction: Take 1 capsule (20 mg) by mouth once daily, 30 minutes before breakfast.

coding.display: adalimumab-bwwd 40 MG in 0.8 ML Prefilled Syringe

note.text: Pending prior authorization from Aetna. Patient has failed two conventional DMARDs: methotrexate (hepatotoxicity) and leflunomide (inadequate response). Continue leflunomide 20 mg daily as background DMARD. Pre-biologic TB screening ordered (TST and QuantiFERON-TB Gold).

coding.display: Rheumatoid arthritis with rheumatoid factor of multiple sites without organ or systems involvement

note.text: Diagnosed per 2010 ACR/EULAR classification criteria. Score >=6 based on: joint involvement (>10 small joints), serology (high-positive RF 78 IU/mL and anti-CCP >250 U/mL), elevated acute phase reactants (ESR 42, CRP 2.8), symptom duration >6 weeks. Early erosive disease on baseline imaging.

3-month methotrexate follow-up: Partial response — DAS28-CRP improved from 5.1 to 4.2 (moderate disease activity range). Inflammatory markers trending down (ESR 42→32, CRP 2.8→1.8). Residual active synovitis in MCPs and wrists. Functional status modestly improved (HAQ-DI 1.2→1.0). Methotrexate dose increased from 15 mg to 20 mg weekly.

code.text: Mild drug-induced hepatotoxicity due to methotrexate
ALT 98 U/L and AST 82 U/L (>2x upper limit of normal)

note.text: LFTs trending toward normalization (ALT 98→38, AST 82→34). Hepatitis B and C serologies previously negative. Methotrexate permanently discontinued; will not be rechallenged given severity of transaminase elevation (>2x ULN).

code.text: ALT 38 U/L (improved from 98), AST 34 U/L (improved from 82)

note.text: New epigastric symptoms in the setting of daily naproxen 500 mg use. No alarm features. Omeprazole 20 mg daily initiated for gastroprotection.

code.text: Functional impairment due to rheumatoid arthritis

note.text: Worsening documented — HAQ-DI increased from 1.2 to 1.5. Patient unable to perform essential occupational tasks: cannot safely demonstrate power tools, has handed off batting practice. Grip strength severely diminished bilaterally (R 22 kg, L 18 kg vs pre-disease baseline >40 kg).

reasonCode.text: Evaluation of symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis with positive serologies; suspected rheumatoid arthritis

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos was seen for initial rheumatology consultation, referred by Dr. Linda Tran at Alamo Family Practice for evaluation of symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis with positive serologies.
Carlos presented for routine lab monitoring 4 weeks after starting methotrexate 15 mg weekly.

reasonCode.text: 3-month methotrexate follow-up for rheumatoid arthritis, disease activity reassessment

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos returned for his 3-month follow-up after initiating methotrexate. He reported notable improvement: morning stiffness reduced from >60 minutes to approximately 30 minutes, less hand swelling, and he was able to perform more shop demonstrations.

reasonCode.text: Routine methotrexate monitoring for seropositive rheumatoid arthritis; mild LFT elevation detected

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for routine lab monitoring on methotrexate 20 mg weekly (started June 2021, 3 months on higher dose).
Carlos presented for follow-up lab monitoring on methotrexate 20 mg weekly (6 months on higher dose).
Dr. Fuentes called Carlos to discuss the November 1 lab results showing ALT 98 and AST 82, more than twice the upper limit of normal.

reasonCode.text: Liver enzyme follow-up, disease flare assessment

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos returned for follow-up 6 weeks after methotrexate discontinuation. He reported that the nausea and fatigue had resolved within 2 weeks of stopping the medication.

reasonCode.text: Initiation of leflunomide as second-line conventional DMARD therapy

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for initiation of leflunomide as second-line conventional DMARD therapy after methotrexate failure due to hepatotoxicity. He reported continued joint stiffness and pain, using naproxen daily.

reasonCode.text: 3-month leflunomide efficacy assessment for rheumatoid arthritis

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos connected via video visit for his 3-month leflunomide efficacy assessment. He reported modest improvement: morning stiffness reduced from >60 minutes to approximately 45 minutes, slight reduction in hand swelling, but still using naproxen daily.

reasonCode.text: 6-month leflunomide efficacy assessment, inadequate response documented

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for his critical 6-month leflunomide efficacy assessment. He was visibly frustrated, reporting that his symptoms had plateaued and possibly worsened over the past 2 months.

reasonCode.text: Follow-up bilateral hand X-rays to assess radiographic progression of rheumatoid arthritis

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for follow-up bilateral hand X-rays ordered by Dr. Fuentes for comparison with the March 2021 baseline to assess for radiographic progression of rheumatoid arthritis.

reasonCode.text: Biologic therapy discussion, prior authorization initiated

extension.valueMarkdown: Carlos presented for a planned visit to discuss biologic therapy escalation and initiate the prior authorization process with Aetna. He reported stable but unsatisfactory symptoms on leflunomide — morning stiffness of 45 minutes daily, continued difficulty with shop demonstrations and coaching activities.

reasonCode.text: Prior authorization status update for Hadlima (adalimumab-bwwd), continued RA management

extension.valueMarkdown: Dr. Fuentes's office called Carlos to provide an update on the Hadlima (adalimumab-bwwd) prior authorization status with Aetna.

code.text: Bilateral hand X-rays (PA and oblique views, comparison to March 2021 baseline)

note.text: Comparison to March 2021 baseline study to document interval radiographic progression. Scheduled for August 2022. Results needed for biologic prior authorization submission.
